assessmentName: Azure Virtual Machine Scale Set Production Optimization

principles: Discover supported metrics, scale set properties, instance state, SKU capabilities, autoscale settings, and prices dynamically before applying rules.
Evaluate the scale set as both a fleet and a set of individual VM instances using VMName and LUN metric dimensions.
Use Azure Cost Management actual cost as the primary current-cost signal.
Use Azure Retail Prices API for target VM size, reservation, savings plan, Spot, operating system, and disk price comparison.
Use Resource SKUs and VMSS model properties to enumerate compatible target VM sizes instead of hardcoding families.
Prefer the best valid production target: tune autoscale, resize instance SKU, change min/max/default capacity, adjust zones, improve health, reserve/savings plan, or secure.
Never recommend a cheaper target that violates production CPU, memory, disk, network, availability, security, image, orchestration, or application scaling constraints.

lowCallCollectionPlan.goal: Collect VMSS model, fleet metrics, autoscale, cost, price, and instance health data with the fewest REST calls while preserving recommendation quality.
callReductionPrinciples: Use subscription or resource-group list APIs instead of one GET per scale set.
Use VMSS-level metrics with VMName and LUN dimensions to avoid listing every instance during the first pass.
Cache metric definitions by resource type, location, namespace, and API version.
Fetch multiple VMSS metric names in one Azure Monitor metrics request using comma-separated metricnames.
Fetch autoscale settings once per scale set only when autoscale evaluation is enabled or the model indicates variable capacity.
Fetch Resource SKUs once per region and cache capabilities for all scale sets in that region.
Use Cost Management grouped by ResourceId once per scope instead of querying cost per scale set or instance.
Use Retail Prices once per region and VM meter group, cache paged results, and reuse for all scale sets in that region.
List VMSS instances or instance views only for scale sets with unhealthy, skewed, saturated, or model-drift signals.

recommendedMetricRequest.singleVmssUrlTemplate: https://management.azure.com/{vmssResourceId}/providers/Microsoft.Insights/metrics?api-version=2023-10-01&metricnamespace=Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachineScaleSets&metricnames=Percentage CPU,Available Memory Percentage,Available Memory Bytes,CPU Credits Consumed,CPU Credits Remaining,Network In Total,Network Out Total,Inbound Flows,Outbound Flows,VmAvailabilityMetric,VM Cached IOPS Consumed Percentage,VM Uncached IOPS Consumed Percentage,VM Cached Bandwidth Consumed Percentage,VM Uncached Bandwidth Consumed Percentage,OS Disk Latency,Data Disk Latency,OS Disk Queue Depth,Data Disk Queue Depth,OS Disk Read Operations/Sec,OS Disk Write Operations/Sec,Data Disk Read Operations/Sec,Data Disk Write Operations/Sec&aggregation=average,minimum,maximum,total&interval={interval}&timespan={start}/{end}
recommendedMetricRequest.dimensionUse: Use VMName and LUN dimensions to find hot, idle, or unhealthy instances before making instance-level calls.

conditionalDrilldownRules.trigger: unhealthy instances, low availability, or failed upgrade signals
conditionalDrilldownRules.reason: Fleet metrics identify the issue; instance view identifies the exact failing nodes and statuses.

conditionalDrilldownRules.trigger: autoscale min, max, oscillation, or missing-autoscale recommendation
conditionalDrilldownRules.reason: Capacity recommendations require current autoscale profile and rule configuration.

conditionalDrilldownRules.trigger: load skew by VMName or LUN
extraCalls: list VMSS instances only if skew cannot be explained by metrics dimensions
conditionalDrilldownRules.reason: Skew can often be detected from metrics; instance calls are reserved for remediation context.

lowCallCollectionPlan.analyzerRuntimeCalls: none; analyzer reads normalized DB records and this rule file

derivedMetrics.fleetP95CpuPct: p95(Percentage CPU across VMName and time)
derivedMetrics.fleetP05AvailableMemoryPct: p05(Available Memory Percentage across VMName and time)
derivedMetrics.fleetP95NetworkInMbps: p95(Network In Total bytes per minute across VMName * 8 / 60 / 1000000)
derivedMetrics.fleetP95NetworkOutMbps: p95(Network Out Total bytes per minute across VMName * 8 / 60 / 1000000)
derivedMetrics.networkSkewRatio: max(instance network throughput) / max(avg instance network throughput, 1)
derivedMetrics.fleetP95CachedIopsConsumedPct: p95(VM Cached IOPS Consumed Percentage across VMName)
derivedMetrics.fleetP95UncachedIopsConsumedPct: p95(VM Uncached IOPS Consumed Percentage across VMName)
derivedMetrics.fleetP95CachedBandwidthConsumedPct: p95(VM Cached Bandwidth Consumed Percentage across VMName)
derivedMetrics.fleetP95UncachedBandwidthConsumedPct: p95(VM Uncached Bandwidth Consumed Percentage across VMName)
derivedMetrics.unhealthyInstancePct: count(instances with unavailable or failed status) / count(total instances) * 100
derivedMetrics.autoscaleAtMinPct: percent of time actual capacity == autoscale minimum
derivedMetrics.autoscaleAtMaxPct: percent of time actual capacity == autoscale maximum
derivedMetrics.scaleOscillationCount: count(scale in/out events within lookback window)
derivedMetrics.cpuCreditRiskPct: count(B-series instances with low CPU Credits Remaining) / count(B-series instances) * 100

requiredProductionFilters: target VM size must be available in the VMSS region and subscription
target VM size must be compatible with orchestration mode, image, OS, architecture, and extensions
target VM size must satisfy per-instance p99 CPU demand plus production buffer
target VM size must satisfy per-instance memory demand plus production buffer
target VM size must satisfy per-instance network, flow, cached disk, and uncached disk requirements

target VM size must support required data disk count, disk size, premium storage, Ultra Disk, accelerated networking, GPU, InfiniBand, ephemeral OS disk, hibernation, Trusted Launch, Confidential VM, or nested virtualization requirements
target capacity or autoscale minimum must preserve application availability and quorum requirements
target zones and placement strategy must preserve required resilience posture
target upgrade policy must preserve production rollout safety
target option must not weaken required security posture
target option must preserve licensing, marketplace plan, capacity reservation, dedicated host, proximity placement, and Spot eviction constraints

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.idleFleetDays') gte 30 and get_value(resource, 'signals.productionCritical') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Reduce default and minimum capacity, add scheduled autoscale, or deallocate/delete the scale set after owner validation.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: For recurring idle windows, prefer scheduled autoscale. For persistent idle non-production fleets, reduce min/default capacity or remove the scale set.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: idleFleetDays >= configuredIdleThreshold AND productionCritical != true

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.autoscaleConfigured') neq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.variableDemand') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable autoscale using CPU, memory, queue, request, or custom application metrics.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use CPU-only autoscale as fallback; prefer workload metrics for queues, request rate, or service latency when available.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: autoscale settings missing AND workload demand varies materially over time

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.autoscaleAtMinPct') gte 80 and get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetP95CpuPct') lt 40

recommendationRules.recommendation: Lower autoscale minimum capacity if availability, quorum, and warm capacity requirements allow.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Choose the smallest min capacity that preserves zone spread, load balancer health, quorum, and recovery objectives.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: autoscaleAtMinPct > 80 AND fleet utilization is low at minimum capacity

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.autoscaleAtMaxPct') gte 20 and get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetP95CpuPct') gte 70

recommendationRules.recommendation: Increase autoscale maximum capacity, resize instances, or scale out across additional scale sets.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: If per-instance saturation is high and at max capacity, increase max first for stateless workloads; resize if every instance is resource-bound.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: autoscaleAtMaxPct > 20 AND fleetP95CpuPct > 70 OR memory/network/disk saturation occurs at max capacity

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.scaleOscillationCount') gte 5

recommendationRules.recommendation: Tune autoscale thresholds, cooldowns, scale step sizes, and separate scale-in from scale-out sensitivity.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Increase cooldown or widen thresholds when repeated scale in/out creates instability.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: scaleOscillationCount exceeds threshold

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetP95CpuPct') lt 40 and get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetAvgAvailableMemoryPct') gt 40 and get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetDiskNetworkUtilizationPct') lt 40

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move the scale set model to the cheapest valid VM size that satisfies per-instance demand, fleet capacity, autoscale, feature, availability, and security requirements.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Compare instance resize versus reducing capacity. Prefer capacity reduction when load is evenly distributed and min capacity is too high; prefer smaller SKU when each instance is consistently over-provisioned.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: fleetP95CpuPct < 40 AND fleetAvgAvailableMemoryPct > 40 AND disk and network utilization are below 40% for 14-30 days

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetP95CpuPct') gte 80

recommendationRules.recommendation: Increase autoscale capacity, resize VM SKU, or scale out behind the load balancer.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: If load is evenly distributed and autoscale has headroom, scale out. If at max capacity, increase max. If every instance is CPU-bound and scale-out is inefficient, resize.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: fleetP95CpuPct > 80 OR many instances have p95CpuPct > 80

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetP05AvailableMemoryPct') lte 10 or get_value(resource, 'signals.minInstanceAvailableMemoryPct') lte 5

recommendationRules.recommendation: Resize to higher-memory VM SKU or reduce per-instance workload density.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Do not solve memory pressure by adding instances unless workload memory state is evenly redistributed by scaling.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: fleetP05AvailableMemoryPct < 10 OR minInstanceAvailableMemoryPct < 5

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.cpuSkewRatio') gte 3 or get_value(resource, 'signals.networkSkewRatio') gte 3

recommendationRules.recommendation: Investigate load balancer distribution, session affinity, application partitioning, hot shards, unhealthy instances, or uneven queue assignment.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Avoid resizing the whole fleet until skew is explained; fix distribution when few instances are hot.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: cpuSkewRatio or networkSkewRatio exceeds threshold

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.usesBSeries') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.cpuCreditRiskPct') gte 20

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move to non-burstable instances or larger B-series if sustained CPU is expected.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Keep B-series only when credits recover and spikes are rare.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: VMSS uses B-series and cpuCreditRiskPct exceeds threshold

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetP95CachedIopsConsumedPct') gte 80 or get_value(resource, 'signals.fleetP95UncachedIopsConsumedPct') gte 80

recommendationRules.recommendation: Resize VM SKU for higher storage limits, adjust disk caching, change disk tier, or rebalance workload.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: If VM-level cached or uncached metrics are saturated, include VM SKU change; if only disk-level LUN is saturated, recommend disk tier/performance change.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: fleetP95CachedIopsConsumedPct > 80 OR fleetP95UncachedIopsConsumedPct > 80 OR fleetP95CachedBandwidthConsumedPct > 80 OR fleetP95UncachedBandwidthConsumedPct > 80 OR disk latency/queue depth exceeds threshold

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.networkUtilizationPct') gte 80

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable accelerated networking where supported, resize to higher network bandwidth, or scale out.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Scale out for stateless services; resize for per-connection or per-node network bottlenecks.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: per-instance network throughput or flows approach target SKU capability

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.baselineCapacitySteady') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.expectedLifetimeDays') gte 365

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate Reserved VM Instances for stable baseline capacity or Azure savings plan for flexible compute usage.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Reserve only baseline min capacity or steady utilization; do not reserve burst capacity that autoscale frequently removes.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: baseline capacity is steady AND expectedLifetimeDays >= 365 AND workload is not Spot-only

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.workloadInterruptible') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.statefulProduction') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate Spot VMSS instances with eviction handling and sufficient on-demand baseline.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use Spot for interruptible workers, batch, CI, rendering, or stateless excess capacity. Keep critical baseline on Regular priority.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: workloadInterruptible == true AND statefulProduction != true

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.productionCritical') eq True and get_value(resource, 'properties.zonesConfigured') eq True and get_value(resource, 'properties.zoneBalance') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable or enforce balanced zone distribution where supported.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use zone balance when application needs even zone capacity and region supports zones.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND zones configured AND zoneBalance != true

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.singlePlacementGroup') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.scaleMayExceedPlacementGroup') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Evaluate disabling single placement group for larger scale requirements.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Consider network topology, fault domains, load balancer, and orchestration mode before changing.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: singlePlacementGroup == true AND scale requirement may exceed placement group limits

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.productionCritical') eq True and get_value(resource, 'properties.automaticRepairsEnabled') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable automatic instance repairs with health probes or application health extension.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Require reliable health signal before enabling repairs.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND automaticRepairsPolicy.enabled != true

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.upgradePolicyRisk') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use rolling or manual upgrade policy with safe batch and unhealthy thresholds for production.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Tune maxBatchInstancePercent, maxUnhealthyInstancePercent, and pauseTimeBetweenBatches to match SLO.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: upgradePolicy.mode is Automatic for sensitive production workload OR rolling policy permits too many unhealthy instances

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.overprovision') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.extensionOrLicenseCostOnOverprovisionedVms') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Set doNotRunExtensionsOnOverprovisionedVMs where appropriate or review overprovision behavior.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Keep overprovision for faster, more reliable scale-out unless extension or license cost is material.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: overprovision == true AND extensions run on overprovisioned VMs OR workload has extension/license cost during provisioning

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.publicExposure') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Remove per-instance public IPs where possible, restrict inbound rules, use load balancer, Application Gateway, Bastion, VPN, private endpoints, or Just-in-Time access.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Requires joining VMSS NIC configuration, NSGs, public IPs, and route data.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: networkInterfaceConfigurations include publicIPAddressConfiguration or broad inbound NSG exposure

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'properties.osType') eq 'Linux' and get_value(resource, 'properties.linuxDisablePasswordAuthentication') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Disable SSH password authentication and require SSH keys or managed access.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Validate operational access path before enforcing.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: osType == 'Linux' AND linuxConfiguration.disablePasswordAuthentication != true

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.securityProfileMeetsPolicy') neq True and get_value(resource, 'policy.requiresTrustedLaunchOrConfidential') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use Trusted Launch or Confidential VM compatible image, SKU, and disk security profile.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Check region, image, VM family, OS, disk encryption, and extension compatibility.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: policy requires Trusted Launch or Confidential VM and securityProfile does not satisfy requirement

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.criticalPatchesAvailable') eq True or get_value(resource, 'signals.patchInstallationFailed') eq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable safe guest patching, automatic OS image upgrades, or scheduled update management based on workload requirements.
recommendationRules.bestActionLogic: Use rolling policy and health checks to avoid fleet-wide disruption.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: patching disabled or instance patch status shows critical/security patches available or failed installation

selectionAlgorithm: Get current VMSS model, SKU capacity, orchestration mode, zones, upgrade policy, repair policy, and VM profile.
List VMSS instances and instance views.
Discover metric definitions for the VMSS resource.
Fetch metrics using VMName and LUN dimensions for the production lookback window.
Fetch autoscale settings and scale event history where available.
Calculate fleet percentiles, per-instance percentiles, skew ratios, saturation metrics, availability, and idle windows.
Fetch current actual VMSS compute, disk, network, and license cost from Cost Management.
Fetch Resource SKUs and retail prices for candidate VM sizes, OS meters, reservations, savings plan, and Spot.
Build candidate actions and target sizes from valid region/subscription SKUs and workload constraints.
Remove candidates that fail production filters.
Score remaining candidates using configured weights.
Return the highest-scoring recommendation with reason, expected impact, risk, rollout guidance, and validation requirements.

bestRecommendation.action: resize | scale_out | scale_in | tune_autoscale | reserve | savings_plan | spot | improve_ha | secure | no_action

knownLimitations: VMSS platform metrics may not include every guest OS, application, queue, or request metric required for final autoscale tuning.
Memory metrics availability can depend on VM agent and metric support; avoid memory-based downsizing if memory data is missing.
Application-level autoscale should prefer workload metrics such as queue length, request rate, latency, or custom SLO metrics when available.
Retail prices are public prices and may not reflect negotiated rates, enterprise discounts, credits, or benefits; current cost should come from Cost Management.
Changing VMSS model size, orchestration settings, upgrade policy, zones, or security profile can require rolling upgrades, reimage, replacement, downtime, quota, or capacity validation.
Network exposure recommendations require joining related resources such as NIC configurations, NSGs, public IPs, load balancers, application gateways, and route tables.
Per-instance cost attribution may require Cost Management grouping and instance metadata; some costs are reported at VMSS or child VM resource granularity depending on meter.

conditionLanguage.fieldPathRoot: normalized DB resource record passed to analyzer
conditionLanguage.unknownHandling: return investigate or cap classification; never mark stay/best from missing required data

classificationLevels.meaning: Already optimized or only minor optional improvements remain.
Generally healthy with low-risk optimization opportunities.
Needs review; measurable cost, performance, reliability, security, or governance risk exists.
Action recommended; resource is materially over-costed, underperforming, risky, or non-compliant.
Immediate action recommended; high waste, outage risk, security exposure, or compliance risk.

evaluationOrder: apply best conditions only when no warning or worst condition matched
apply scoring caps for missing data, security findings, reliability findings, and unknown ownership
return score, classification, matchedConditions, and deterministicRecommendations
notes: Do not parse recommendationRules.condition at runtime; those are descriptive only.
Python assessment must use pythonAssessment.deterministicCases plus normalized metrics/signals.
All runtime decisions must be reproducible from input JSON and this file.

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Current tier/size/configuration is acceptable; keep it and continue monitoring.
validWhen: required cost and metrics are present

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a smaller/lower/cooler/cheaper SKU, tier, capacity, retention, or feature set while preserving production constraints.
validWhen: paid capacity or premium feature is unused
target capability satisfies observed demand and policy

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Move to a larger/higher/more resilient SKU, tier, capacity, throughput, or feature set because current configuration is constrained or risky.
validWhen: availability or security baseline requires a higher capability

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Stop, deallocate, archive, delete, expire, or remove the resource or child cost item after owner approval.
validWhen: resource is unattached, idle, expired, duplicate, orphaned, or non-production with no usage

recommendationActionOutcomes.meaning: Do not resize automatically; collect missing data, resolve conflicting signals, or review owner/business context.

pythonAssessment.deterministicCasesSource: legacy scoring support; recommendation runtime should prefer assessmentRules/actionOutcomeRules evaluated against DB fields

runtimeContract.ruleSignalConvention: signals.rules.<recommendationRule.id> == true triggers that recommendation rule
collectorResponsibility: Collect Azure resource properties, Azure Monitor metrics, Cost Management rows, price cache, tags, policy, and advisor data outside recommendation runtime.
Store normalized values in the database using the roots resource, properties, metrics, cost, prices, tags, policy, advisor, and signals.
Calculate derived signals deterministically from stored DB values where raw metrics/properties need aggregation.
Do not require the analyzer to call Azure REST APIs while producing recommendations.
analyzerResponsibility: Load the resource assessment JSON by resourceType.
Load the normalized resource record from the database.
Evaluate rule condition groups with supported operators only.
Return matched outputs including recommendationAction/actionOutcome, message, severity, pillar, resultCode, and evidence.

analyzerContract.purpose: FastAPI/backend analyzer loads this JSON as deterministic optimization rules and evaluates them against metrics/properties/cost already stored in the database.

normalizedInputRecord.resource: identity and inventory fields such as id, name, type, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location, sku, kind, tags, and lifecycle timestamps
normalizedInputRecord.properties: latest ARM/Resource Graph properties stored by collector
normalizedInputRecord.metrics: Azure Monitor metric aggregates stored by collector; expected aggregate suffixes include Avg, Min, Max, P50, P90, P95, P99, Count, Total, TrendPct, and WindowDays where available
normalizedInputRecord.cost: Cost Management actual/amortized/forecast rows normalized by resourceId, meter, skuName, product, chargeType, pricingModel, reservation/savings-plan coverage, and period totals
normalizedInputRecord.prices: optional Retail Prices/API or internal price cache for candidate comparison
normalizedInputRecord.policy: environment, production flag, criticality, required baseline, business hours, retention, and compliance requirements
normalizedInputRecord.signals: derived booleans/numbers calculated by collector/analyzer from DB metrics, properties, cost, and policy

conditionEvaluation.numericComparisonBehavior: compare only when stored value is numeric; otherwise rule_not_applicable
conditionEvaluation.stringComparisonBehavior: case_insensitive for contains/in/not_in when analyzer normalizes strings
conditionEvaluation.arrayComparisonBehavior: contains checks scalar membership or substring membership depending on stored value type
conditionEvaluation.booleanComparisonBehavior: accept true/false booleans; analyzer may normalize string booleans before evaluation

evaluationOrder: load resource-specific assessment file by resourceType
read one normalized resource record from DB with resource/properties/metrics/cost/tags/policy/signals/prices/advisor roots
evaluate data completeness and missing-cost rules
evaluate stop_or_delete rules before resize rules
evaluate upgrade rules before downgrade when both saturation and low-utilization signals conflict
evaluate stay rules only when no higher-priority action matched
return matched rule outputs with recommendationAction/actionOutcome and evidence paths

outputContract.actionFieldMeaning: normalized machine action outcome, one of stay/downgrade/upgrade/stop_or_delete/investigate
